content.text:
Comment class: process-change (owner-triggered merge, completed)

**Merged.** PR #396 is squashed onto `main` as `85dd3dd`, local `main` is synced, and the conversation (1493 entries) is captured to the `conversation-logs` branch tagged to #394.

Because #394 is the multi-phase tracker, the PR used `Refs #394` (not `Closes`), so the tracker stays open for Phases 1–4. CI (`test-and-conformance`) was green on the head commit before merge.

> ✅ **Phase 0 is landed on `main`.** Per-project scheduling's engine, groundwork, checks, task conversions, and cutover guard are merged; nothing changes behavior until a repo declares the `schedule` key. Tracker #394 remains open for the GCEC pilot (Phase 1) and beyond — say the word when you want to start it.

content.text:
Yes — there are a handful, and they matter for a Phase 1 session picking this up. They split into **deviations from the written plan** (someone reading DESIGN/MIGRATION would otherwise expect something different) and **carry-forward gaps** (things the docs assume exist that don't yet). Here's my assessment:

**Deviations to record** (mostly MIGRATION, one DESIGN):
1. **No `scheduled-tasks` pack.** The two checks (`scheduler-workflow-shape`, `task-declaration-shape`) live in **basics**, doctrine in `packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md`. A follow-up hunting for a dedicated pack won't find one — and this is deliberate (scheduling is baseline discipline, active everywhere basics is, so consumers need no extra declaration).
2. **Conversions are additive, not a rename.** DESIGN §1 says "`run_daily/` is *renamed* to `tasks/`" — but Phase 0 landed `tasks/` **alongside** an untouched `run_daily/` (and `pack.mjs` unchanged; the scheduler discovers `tasks/` from disk). The legacy planner keeps reading `run_daily/`; it's deleted only at Phase 4. This is what makes Phase 0 truly behavior-preserving, and it means the "`run_daily`/`tasks` alias" the plan anticipated (0.6) wasn't needed as code.
3. **Two 0.4 rescopes intentionally deferred to Phase 1 cutover** (`gha/no-scheduled-fleet-executor` rescope, the `chrome-extension-release` cron flip) — per the migration's own "lands with the de-cron'd stub" rule. And `in-session-github-access` needed **no** change (already excludes `engine/`).

**Carry-forward gaps** (the important ones for Phase 1):
4. **The repo-hashed :10–:50 cron minute isn't computed anywhere.** The stub ships a placeholder (`10`) with a "bootstrap rewrites this" comment — but no minute-hasher exists (`schedule.mjs` has a 0–6 weekday bucket, not a minute). **Phase 1's GCEC cutover must implement this**, or every repo lands on minute 10 and the anti-stampede design fails.
5. **`store-release`'s inline worker is trigger-only** — the trigger-and-await is a documented stub.
6. **The `fleet` signal collector is a stub** (null for consumers); its `FLEET_GITHUB_TOKEN` wiring is Phase 2.
7. **`run.mjs` + collectors are unit-tested only, never run against live GitHub** — GCEC (Phase 1) is the first live exercise.

One mechanics note: since PR #396 is merged, updating the docs is a **fresh branch off `main`**, not a reopen.

> ⚠️ **My recommendation:** add a short "**Phase 0 — landed**" status block to `MIGRATION.md` capturing items 1–7, plus a one-line clarification in `DESIGN.md §1` that `tasks/` lands additively (legacy `run_daily/` removed at Phase 4) and the checks live in basics. Want me to open that as a fresh PR? It's the difference between a Phase 1 session re-deriving all of the above versus reading it in one place.
I'll update both docs. Since PR #396 is merged, this is a fresh change — restarting the branch from latest `main`:

content.text: I'll insert a "Phase 0 — landed" status block right after the Phase 0 heading:

input.old_string:
## Phase 0 — canon groundwork (Claudinite PRs; no behavior change anywhere)

1. **Build `engine/scheduler/`** (vendored to consumers with the engine):
input.new_string:
## Phase 0 — canon groundwork (Claudinite PRs; no behavior change anywhere)

> **Landed** (#396, on `main`). All seven sub-items below are implemented, with
> the full engine, the rename, the checks, the additive task conversions, the
> cutover guard, and the docs — CI green, no behavior change until a repo
> declares `schedule`. A few things landed **differently from the wording below**,
> and a few are deliberately **carried forward** — a Phase 1 session should read
> these before starting the GCEC pilot:
>
> **Deviations from this plan (as-built):**
> - **No `scheduled-tasks` pack.** The two new checks (`scheduler-workflow-shape`,
>   `task-declaration-shape`) live in the **basics** pack, with the doctrine in
>   [`packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md`](../../packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md).
>   Scheduling is baseline discipline, active wherever basics is declared
>   (everywhere) — so a consumer needs no separate declaration to get the guards.
> - **Task conversions are ADDITIVE, not a rename.** Each canon pack now carries
>   `tasks/<name>/` **alongside** an untouched `run_daily/`; `pack.mjs` is
>   unchanged (the scheduler discovers `tasks/` from disk). The legacy planner
>   keeps reading `run_daily/`; it is deleted only at Phase 4. This is what makes
>   Phase 0 truly behavior-preserving, and it means the `run_daily`/`tasks` alias
>   (0.6) needed no code — both trees simply coexist.
> - **`in-session-github-access` needed no change** — it already scopes to
>   `routines/`, `migrations/`, `run_daily/`, so `engine/scheduler/`'s
>   `GITHUB_TOKEN` use is out of scope for free.
> - **Two 0.4 rescopes are deferred to the Phase 1 cutover** (per this doc's own
>   check/stub ordering rule): the `gha/no-scheduled-fleet-executor` rescope and
>   the `chrome-extension-release` cron flip travel with the de-cron'd stub in a
>   consumer's mount — never before it, or they flag a live workflow. **Not yet
>   done.**
>
> **Carry-forward gaps a Phase 1 session must close:**
> - **The repo-hashed :10–:50 cron minute is not computed anywhere.** The stub
>   ships a placeholder (`cron: '10 * * * *'`) with a "bootstrap rewrites this"
>   comment, but no minute-hasher exists (`routines/fleet/schedule.mjs` has a 0–6
>   weekday bucket, not a minute). **GCEC cutover must implement the hasher**, or
>   every repo lands on minute 10 and the anti-stampede design fails.
> - **`store-release`'s inline `worker.mjs` is trigger-only** — it fires the daily
>   release workflow but does not yet await it (documented stub in the file).
> - **The `fleet` signal collector is a stub** (returns null for consumers); its
>   `FLEET_GITHUB_TOKEN` wiring on canon/sheepdog is Phase 2.
> - **`run.mjs` + the collectors are unit-tested only, never run against live
>   GitHub.** GCEC (Phase 1) is the first live exercise — expect first-run
>   surprises in the ledger read, the issue search/create, and inline execution.
> - **Baselining's remit now explicitly covers scheduler wiring** (its `task.md`
>   re-converges a drifted `claudinite-scheduler.yml`, preserving the repo-hashed
>   minute, and re-creates missing dispatch labels) — a refinement, not a gap.

1. **Build `engine/scheduler/`** (vendored to consumers with the engine):

content.text: Now the DESIGN.md §1 clarification. Let me read the relevant lines:

content.content:
17	## 1. Task anatomy — the pack folder
18	
19	`run_daily/` is renamed to `tasks/`, one directory per task, in canon packs and
20	local packs alike:
21	
22	```
23	packs/<pack>/tasks/<task-name>/
24	  task.mjs        # declaration + precondition — self-contained, importable standalone
25	  task.md         # the worker spec the agent executes; the dispatch issue's first line points here
26	  *.sh, *.js      # optional deterministic helper scripts (the routine-folder convention, absorbed)
27	```
28

file.content:
## 1. Task anatomy — the pack folder

`run_daily/` is renamed to `tasks/`, one directory per task, in canon packs and
local packs alike:

```
packs/<pack>/tasks/<task-name>/
  task.mjs        # declaration + precondition — self-contained, importable standalone
  task.md         # the worker spec the agent executes; the dispatch issue's first line points here
  *.sh, *.js      # optional deterministic helper scripts (the routine-folder convention, absorbed)
```

input.new_string:
## 1. Task anatomy — the pack folder

`run_daily/` is superseded by `tasks/`, one directory per task, in canon packs
and local packs alike:

```
packs/<pack>/tasks/<task-name>/
  task.mjs        # declaration + precondition — self-contained, importable standalone
  task.md         # the worker spec the agent executes; the dispatch issue's first line points here
  *.sh, *.js      # optional deterministic helper scripts (the routine-folder convention, absorbed)
```

> **As-built (Phase 0, #396):** `tasks/` lands **additively** — a canon pack
> carries both `tasks/` and its untouched `run_daily/` through the rollout, so
> the legacy central planner keeps working while the scheduler discovers `tasks/`
> from disk (`pack.mjs` is not touched). `run_daily/` is removed only at Phase 4.
> The two conformance guards for this shape (`task-declaration-shape`,
> `scheduler-workflow-shape`) live in the **basics** pack, not a separate one —
> scheduling is baseline discipline (see
> [`packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md`](../../packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md)).

content.text: Branch pushed fresh. Now opening a new PR for the doc update:

input.head: claude/actions-executor-1hte9p
input.title: docs: record Phase 0 as-built deviations and carry-forward gaps
input.body:
Follow-up to the merged Phase 0 (#396): capture what landed **differently from the written plan** and what's **deliberately carried forward**, so a Phase 1 session can pick up the GCEC pilot without re-deriving it all.

## `MIGRATION.md` — a "Phase 0 landed" status block

**Deviations (as-built):**
- **No `scheduled-tasks` pack** — the two checks (`scheduler-workflow-shape`, `task-declaration-shape`) live in **basics** (doctrine in `packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md`); scheduling is baseline discipline, active everywhere basics is.
- **Task conversions are additive** — `tasks/` beside an untouched `run_daily/`; `pack.mjs` unchanged (scheduler discovers `tasks/` from disk). `run_daily/` removed at Phase 4; the `run_daily`/`tasks` alias needed no code.
- **`in-session-github-access` needed no change** (already excludes `engine/`).
- **Two 0.4 rescopes deferred to Phase 1 cutover** (`no-scheduled-fleet-executor` rescope + `chrome-extension-release` cron flip) — they travel with the de-cron'd stub, per the migration's own ordering rule.

**Carry-forward gaps for Phase 1:**
- **The repo-hashed :10–:50 cron minute is not computed anywhere** — the stub ships a placeholder; GCEC cutover must build the hasher or the anti-stampede design fails.
- `store-release`'s inline worker is trigger-only (no await yet).
- The `fleet` signal collector is a stub (Phase 2 wiring).
- `run.mjs` + collectors are unit-tested only, never run against live GitHub.

## `DESIGN.md` §1

Notes that `tasks/` lands additively (legacy `run_daily/` removed at Phase 4) and the guards live in basics, pointing at the doctrine doc.

Docs-only; conformance clean.

Refs #394

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.text: {"id":"4110146753","url":"https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/pull/398"}

Done. The docs now carry the Phase 0 as-built record, opened as **PR #398** (a fresh branch off `main`, since #396 is merged).

What I recorded, where a Phase 1 session will find it:
- **`MIGRATION.md`** — a "Phase 0 landed" block under the Phase 0 heading, splitting the four **deviations** (no `scheduled-tasks` pack; additive conversions; `in-session-github-access` unchanged; two rescopes deferred) from the four **carry-forward gaps** (the uncomputed cron-minute hasher, the trigger-only release worker, the stub `fleet` collector, the never-run-live CLI).
- **`DESIGN.md §1`** — a short as-built note that `tasks/` lands additively and the guards live in basics.

The single most important line for whoever takes Phase 1: **the repo-hashed :10–:50 cron minute has no implementation yet** — the stub is a placeholder, and GCEC cutover has to build the hasher or every repo collides on minute 10.

> ✅ **Docs updated and pushed as PR #398.** The Phase 0 as-built state and the Phase 1 carry-forwards are now written down in `MIGRATION.md` and `DESIGN.md` — a follow-up session can start from that block. Say "LGTM" on #398 whenever you want it on `main`.
